mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real GDP growth indicates a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and industrial production decline fails to precede SPX drawdowns in more than 40% of historical episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% in a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, rising consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when CPIAUCSL rises alongside FEDFUNDS, or the combined signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates positively with SPX returns in less than 40% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals weakening consumer confidence and future construction activity, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where housing starts breach the threshold, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When rising unemployment coincides with a federal funds rate above 4.0 percent, the dual pressure of slack labor markets and restrictive credit conditions forces a sharp repricing of risk assets, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction coefficient between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.5 percent indicates robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with SPX returns in more than 45% of observations, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 3-year rolling window.
